mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor income and aggregate consumption, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency following payroll gains falls under 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above five percent reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and slows corporate investment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and rise in the unemployment rate signal collapsing consumer demand and rising recession risk, jointly depressing S&P 500 prices over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative returns in more than 50% of triggered 20-day windows, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during a monetary tightening cycle confirms that restrictive policy is cooling the labor market, causing investors to price a hard landing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening cycles, UNRATE increases coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts exceeding five percent signals deteriorating construction activity and negative household wealth effects, reducing aggregate demand and pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day return frequency when HOUST declines > 5% is below 50% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding three and a half percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain elevated real policy rates, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding one percent over two months confirms supply-side recovery and rising capacity utilization, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return frequency following INDPRO increases > 1% is not significantly above baseline (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with decelerating payroll growth reveals monetary policy lag hitting employment, a late-cycle interaction that foreshadows negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases over 3 months while PAYEMS growth slows below 1% annualized
failure_condition: The interaction term does not significantly predict negative 60-day returns (t-stat > -1.5) or the signal's hit rate is below 50%.
